title: Jayson Tatum looked like a star again as Celtics roll past Hornets: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum led the Celtics to a 114-99 victory over the Hornets with 32 points, 8 assists, and 5 three-pointers.
Payton Pritchard contributed 28 points and 6 assists, capitalizing on defensive matchups during key moments in the game.
Ron Harper Jr. had a strong first half performance but twisted his ankle in the second quarter; he later returned but was quieter.

title: Jaylen Brown boasts on social media after Celtics clinch playoff berth for 12th straight season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown commented '50 wins in a gap year' on social media after Celtics' playoff berth by defeating the Hornets.
The Celtics secured the Eastern Conference playoff spot for the 12th consecutive year, extending the NBA's longest-active streak.
Jayson Tatum scored a season-high 32 points despite his recent return from an Achilles injury, leading Boston to victory.

title: Connelly Early continues to shine for Red Sox after strong first start in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Connelly Early pitched 5.1 innings, allowing one run on five hits, with six strikeouts in Red Sox's 3-2 loss to the Reds.
Early, 23, has a 2.19 ERA over five career MLB starts, showing potential as a stable option in Boston's rotation.
Initially outside the rotation, Early started due to Ranger Suarez's delay and Oviedo's struggles, impressing in his first outing.

title: Boston Fleet cinch berth in Walter Cup Playoffs with win over Minnesota Frost
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Fleet secured a Walter Cup Playoff spot with a 4-2 win over Minnesota Frost at Grand Casino Arena.
Fleet became the first team to clinch a 2026 playoff berth, setting a league record by earning it fastest in history.
Rookie Sophie Shirley scored twice, while Amanda Thiele made 21 saves in her debut for Boston Fleet.

title: Woman charged in fatal South Boston stabbing
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Gisselle Pascual, 24, charged with manslaughter after a fatal stabbing in South Boston, police report.
Victim found at 258 Old Colony Ave with a stab wound, died in hospital the following day.
Pascual's charges upgraded from assault after victim's death; arraignment in South Boston District Court pending.

title: Brockton police shoot man who allegedly fired at officers, man sleeping nearby also injured, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Brockton police shot a 44-year-old man in the shoulder after he allegedly fired at officers following a car pursuit.
A 71-year-old man sleeping in a nearby home was grazed by a bullet during the exchange of gunfire, officials said.
Massachusetts State Police are investigating the incident, and a handgun with an obliterated serial number was recovered from the scene.

title: Former Bruins coach Bruce Cassidy abruptly fired by Vegas before playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruce Cassidy was fired by the Vegas Golden Knights before the Stanley Cup Playoffs, replaced by John Tortorella.
Cassidy led Vegas to its first Stanley Cup in 2022-23 and had a 178-99-43 record over four seasons.
Vegas struggled this season, partly due to poor goalie performances, and holds a 36-26-16 record in the Pacific Division.

title: New NFL mock draft has Patriots addressing major need on defense with 31st pick
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: NFL.com's mock draft has Patriots selecting Clemson DE T.J. Parker with the 31st overall pick.
Patriots aim to bolster defense; Parker recorded 21.5 sacks and 126 tackles in college.
K'Lavon Chaisson departed in free agency; Dre’Mont Jones signed, but more pass rush help needed.

title: North Shore police department asking for public’s help after theft of historical plaques
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A historical plaque was stolen from the Old Hill graveyard in Newburyport on March 24, highlighting African American history.
The theft is part of a series of recent bronze plaque thefts in Newburyport, including locations like Oak Hill Cemetery.
Newburyport police request public assistance and information on the thefts; contact them at 978-462-4411.

title: Bruins sign Merrimack goalie Max Lundgren to entry-level contract
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins signed Merrimack College goalie Max Lundgren to a one-year, entry-level contract for the 2026-27 season.
Lundgren, 23, achieved a 21-16-2 record, 2.55 GAA, and .920 save percentage during the 2025-26 season at Merrimack.
Lundgren earned MVP honors in the Hockey East tournament, helping Merrimack to its first title with a shutout in the semifinals.
